title: pilea aquarum subsp. brevicornuta
intro: Pilea aquarum subsp. brevicornuta is a subspecies of the Pilea aquarum plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. Native to tropical regions, this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Pilea aquarum subsp. brevicornuta is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
